svg.text: CSL — CHINESE SIGN LANGUAGE
GRAMMAR — EAST ASIAN SIGN SYNTAX
CSL WORD ORDER
CSL: primarily SOV word order (mirrors Mandarin Chinese)
PERSON BOOK GIVE: "Person gives book" (SOV structure)
Topic-comment also very frequent (matching Chinese spoken style)
Time signed first (same as spoken Chinese: time-topic then action)
CSL VERB SYSTEM
CSL: aspect system (same as all sign languages; no tense morphology)
Movement modification: repeated = habitual; slow = prolonged
Spatial verb agreement: directional verbs move between loci
CSL CLASSIFIERS
CSL: rich classifier system (proforms) like other sign languages
Some classifiers: trace shapes of Chinese characters (iconic + conventional)
CSL NEGATION
Headshake: simultaneous negative NMM (same as all sign languages)
NO/NOT sign also used; combined = emphatic negation
KSL GRAMMAR · SECONDARY LANGUAGE
KSL WORD ORDER: SOV
KSL: SOV word order (mirrors Korean spoken grammar)
PERSON BOOK GIVE: same SOV order as CSL
Korean spoken language: SOV • KSL: SOV (parallel influence)
KSL UNIQUE GRAMMAR FEATURES
KSL: rich use of spatial grammar (loci, agreement verbs)
Some Korean honorific system reflected in KSL (like Korean spoken)
Politeness distinctions: some signs have formal/informal variants
GRAMMAR COMPARISON
Both CSL and KSL: SOV (reflecting their respective spoken languages)
Both: topic-comment • both: aspect over tense
Both: spatial grammar • both: classifiers
SOV sign languages: DGS, CSL, KSL • SVO sign languages: ASL, LSF, BSL
PLATE 65c ALT · CSL / KSL · GRAMMAR
